title: The Key Prop Explained Deeply
description: Why keys are essential for performance and correctness.

slides.title: What is a Key? 🔑
slides.content:
# Keys Help React Identify Items

A **key** is a special string attribute you need to include when creating lists of elements. Keys give each element a stable identity, so React can track changes (additions, removals, reorders) efficiently.

## Key Requirements

* Keys must be **unique among siblings** (but globally they can be duplicate).
* Keys must be **stable** – they shouldn't change between renders.

## Where to Put Keys

```jsx
{data.map(item => (
  <ListItem key={item.id} item={item} />
))}
```

React uses keys during the **reconciliation** (diffing) process. Without keys, React would have to re-render every item even if only one changed.

slides.title: Correct Key Usage
slides.content: Always use a unique and stable identifier from your data, if available:

quizData.question: What is the primary purpose of the `key` prop in lists?

options.text: To give React a way to identify items during updates

quizData.explanation: Keys help React identify which items have changed, been added, or removed, enabling efficient updates.
